briefdescription.para: AES BLOCK SIZE of 16 bytes or 128 bits.
Enumeration defines the AES modes supported by the SI91X device.

detaileddescription.para: This enumeration defines different AES modes supported by the SI91X device, such as AES CBC, AES ECB, and AES CTR modes.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration defines the AES encryption and decryption types.

detaileddescription.para: This enumeration defines the types of AES operations supported, which includes encryption and decryption.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration defines the key sizes supported by the AES encryption algorithm on the SI91X device, which includes 128-bit, 192-bit, and 256-bit key sizes.
To encrypt or decrypt the message according to the given configuration.

detaileddescription.para:
This is a blocking API.
config
Configuration object of type sl_si91x_aes_config_t
output
Buffer to store the output.
The maximum length of the message in bytes that can be handled in one go is determined by SLI_SI91X_MAX_DATA_SIZE_IN_BYTES_FOR_AES. If the message length is not a multiple of SL_SI91X_AES_BLOCK_SIZE, the function returns an error.
sl_status_t. For more information on status codes, refer to SL STATUS DOCUMENTATION.

memberdef.argsstring: (const sl_si91x_aes_config_t *config, uint16_t chunk_length, uint8_t aes_flags, uint8_t *output)
memberdef.name: sl_si91x_aes_multipart

briefdescription.para: Encrypts or decrypts a chunk of a message (CBC and ECB modes are not supported for multipart operations) according to the given configuration.

detaileddescription.para:
This is a blocking API.
config
Configuration object of type sl_si91x_aes_config_t containing the AES operation parameters.
chunk_length
Length of the current chunk to be processed in bytes.
aes_flags
Flags indicating the position of the current chunk. Valid values are:- SL_SI91X_AES_FIRST_CHUNK, SL_SI91X_AES_MIDDLE_CHUNK, SL_SI91X_AES_LAST_CHUNK. Buffer to store the processed output data.
sl_status_t. For more information on status codes, refer to SL STATUS DOCUMENTATION.
The maximum length of the message in bytes that can be handled in one go is determined by SLI_SI91X_MAX_DATA_SIZE_IN_BYTES_FOR_AES.
If the message length exceeds this limit, the function returns SL_STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER.
If the message length is not a multiple of SL_SI91X_AES_BLOCK_SIZE, the function returns an error.

listitem.para: The origin of this software must not be misrepresented; you must not claim that you wrote the original software. If you use this software in a product, an acknowledgment in the product documentation would be appreciated but is not required.
Altered source versions must be plainly marked as such, and must not be misrepresented as being the original software.
This notice may not be removed or altered from any source distribution.
